database_metadata.name: Airline
database_metadata.description: Airline on-time data are reported each month to the U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT), Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) by the 16 U.S. air carriers that have at least 1 percent of total domestic scheduled-service passenger revenues, plus two other carriers that report voluntarily. The data cover nonstop scheduled-service flights between points within the United States (including territories) as described in 14 CFR Part 234 of DOT's regulations.

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Directly search for entities or records
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Return the names of all users who have purchased the iPhone 13.
Which city had the highest average order value over the past 6 months?

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Multi-table joins, multi-hop logic, existence/exclusion relationships
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Which users have placed orders but never left any product reviews on the platform?

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Inferring future behavior or state based on historical data
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Based on historical behavior, which users are unlikely to place another order within the next 30 days?

tables.description: Airline description string, usually in the form 'Carrier Name: Code'. This field combines the airline name and a short carrier code in one text value.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the airline lookup table; a numeric airline/carrier identifier.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the airline entry, typically in the form 'Airline Name: Carrier Code'. This field combines the carrier name and a short airline/carrier code in one string.

random_samples.description: Virgin Blue International Airlines t/a V Australia: VA

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (1610 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for airport codes used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each airport code to a textual airport description, typically including city, state/territory abbreviation, and airport name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the airport lookup table; an airport code used in BTS airline data.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the airport entry, typically in the form 'City, State: Airport Name'. This field combines location information and the airport name in one string.

random_samples.description: Mont Laurier, Canada: Mont Laurier Airport
Blue Fox Bay, AK: Blue Fox Bay Airport
Circle Hot Springs, AK: Circle Hot Springs Airport
Monument Valley, UT: Monument Valley Airport
Freeport, The Bahamas: Grand Bahama International
Richards Bay, South Africa: Richards Bay Airport

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (6385 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for U.S. DOT airport identifiers used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each numeric airport identifier to a textual airport description, typically including city, state/territory abbreviation, and airport name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the airport-ID lookup table; a U.S. DOT airport identifier corresponding to fields such as `OriginAirportID` or `DestAirportID`. It uniquely identifies an airport across years, even if airport codes change or are reused.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the airport entry, typically in the form 'City, State: Airport Name'. This field combines location information and the airport name in one string.

random_samples.description: Cat Lake, Canada: Cat Lake Airport
Fire Island, AK: Fire Island Airport
Avon Park, FL: Avon Park Executive

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (6370 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for U.S. DOT airport sequence identifiers used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each time-specific airport sequence identifier to a textual airport description, typically including city, state/territory abbreviation, and airport name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the airport-sequence-ID lookup table; a U.S. DOT airport sequence identifier corresponding to fields such as `OriginAirportSeqID` or `DestAirportSeqID`. It identifies a unique airport at a given point in time, allowing airport attributes such as name or coordinates to change over time.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the airport entry, typically in the form 'City, State: Airport Name'. This field combines location information and the airport name in one string.

random_samples.description: Bissau, Guinea-Bissau: Bissau Oswaldo Vieira International
King Khalid Military City, Saudi Arabia: King Saud AB
McDonald Lake, AK: McDonald Lake Airport

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (13295 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for flight cancellation reasons in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each cancellation code to its corresponding cancellation category.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the cancellation lookup table; a one-letter cancellation reason code used in the flight fact table.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text label of the cancellation reason category. In this dataset, the valid values are: A = Carrier, B = Weather, C = National Air System, and D = Security.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for U.S. DOT city market identifiers used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each city market identifier to a textual city-market description, typically including city and state/territory.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the city-market lookup table; a U.S. DOT city market identifier corresponding to fields such as `OriginCityMarketID` or `DestCityMarketID`. It is used to consolidate airports serving the same city market.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the city market, typically in the form 'City, State'.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (5762 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for scheduled departure and arrival time blocks in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each time-block code to a human-readable hourly time interval description.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the departure/arrival time-block lookup table; a scheduled time-block code such as `0600-0659` or `2300-2359` used by fields like `DepTimeBlk` and `ArrTimeBlk`.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable description of the scheduled time block, typically expressing the corresponding local-time interval, such as `6:00AM to 6:59AM`.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for flight distance groups in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each distance-group code to a human-readable flight-distance interval based on 250-mile bins.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the distance-group lookup table; an integer code used by the `DistanceGroup` field to represent a flight-distance interval.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable description of the flight-distance interval associated with the distance-group code. The intervals are defined in 250-mile bins, such as '250-499 Miles' or '2500 Miles and Greater'.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for diversion outcomes in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each diversion-related code to a textual description of the number of diverted-airport landings or a special diversion outcome.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the diversion lookup table; a diversion-related code representing the number of diverted airport landings or a special return/cancellation outcome.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Text description of the diversion outcome. Codes 0-5 represent the number of diverted airport landings, while code 9 indicates an air return to the origin airport where the flight was ultimately cancelled.

full_enum.description: Air Return to Origin Airport where the Flight was Ultimately Cancelled

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for calendar months used in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each numeric month code to its corresponding month name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the month lookup table; an integer month code from 1 to 12.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable month name corresponding to the month code, such as `January` for 1 and `December` for 12.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for on-time delay groups in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each delay-group code to a human-readable delay interval description used for grouped departure and arrival delay analysis.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the on-time delay-group lookup table; an integer code representing a grouped delay interval used by fields such as `DepartureDelayGroups` and `ArrivalDelayGroups`.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable description of the delay interval associated with the delay-group code. Negative codes represent early departures/arrivals, 0 represents delays from 0 to 14 minutes, and positive codes represent progressively larger delay intervals in 15-minute bins, with 12 meaning delays of 180 minutes or more.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for calendar quarters used in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each numeric quarter code to its corresponding quarter label and calendar date range.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the quarter lookup table; an integer quarter code from 1 to 4 corresponding to the `Quarter` field.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable quarter label and date range corresponding to the quarter code, such as `Quarter1: January 1-March 31`.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for aviation-related state or province abbreviations used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each two-letter location abbreviation to a human-readable state, district, or Canadian province name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the state-abbreviation lookup table; a two-letter aviation location abbreviation used by fields such as `OriginState` or `DestState`.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable name corresponding to the location abbreviation. The values include U.S. states and districts, and may also include Canadian provinces for aviation-related geographic coding.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (67 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for state FIPS codes used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each numeric FIPS location code to its corresponding state or territory name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the state-FIPS lookup table; a numeric FIPS location code used by fields such as `OriginStateFips` or `DestStateFips`.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable name corresponding to the FIPS location code. Values include U.S. states, the District of Columbia, territories, and special values such as `0 = Not Applicable`.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (74 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for BTS unique carrier codes used in the Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each unique carrier code to the corresponding carrier name.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the unique-carrier lookup table; a BTS unique carrier code corresponding to the `Reporting_Airline` field. This code is intended for analysis across years. When the same code has been reused by multiple carriers over time, BTS adds a numeric suffix to earlier users to keep the code unique.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable carrier name corresponding to the unique carrier code.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (1610 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for weekday codes used in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each numeric weekday code to its corresponding weekday name, with an additional code for unknown values.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the weekday lookup table; an integer weekday code used by the `DayOfWeek` field. Codes 1-7 represent Monday through Sunday, and 9 represents an unknown value.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable weekday label corresponding to the weekday code, such as `Monday` for 1 and `Sunday` for 7; `Unknown` is used for code 9.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Lookup table for World Area Codes (WAC) used in the BTS airline datasets. It maps each numeric WAC code to a human-readable geographic area name used by fields such as `OriginWac` and `DestWac`.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the World Area Code lookup table; a numeric WAC code used by fields such as `OriginWac` and `DestWac` to identify a geographic area.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable name corresponding to the World Area Code. Values may represent U.S. states, territories, or other aviation-related geographic areas.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (333 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Lookup table for binary yes/no response codes used in the BTS Airline On-Time Performance data. It maps each numeric response code to its corresponding yes/no label.

columns.name: code
columns.comment: Primary key of the yes/no response lookup table; a binary response code where 0 means `No` and 1 means `Yes`.

columns.name: description
columns.comment: Human-readable yes/no label corresponding to the response code.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Flight-level fact table for U.S. domestic reporting-carrier on-time performance in January 2016. Each row represents one scheduled nonstop flight operation and includes calendar fields, carrier identifiers, origin and destination attributes, departure and arrival performance, cancellation and diversion indicators, elapsed-time summaries, and delay-cause measures.

columns.name: year
columns.comment: Calendar year of the flight record.

columns.name: quarter
columns.comment: Calendar quarter of the flight record (1-4).

columns.name: month
columns.comment: Calendar month of the flight record.

columns.name: dayofmonth
columns.comment: Day of month of the flight record.

columns.name: dayofweek
columns.comment: Day of week of the flight record.

columns.name: flightdate
columns.comment: Flight date.

columns.name: uniquecarrier
columns.comment: BTS unique carrier code for the reporting airline. BTS recommends this field for analysis across years because reused carrier codes are disambiguated with suffixes.

columns.name: airlineid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT identifier for the reporting airline (carrier); foreign key to `l_airline_id.code`.

columns.name: carrier
columns.comment: IATA carrier code assigned to the reporting airline. BTS notes that this code is not always unique across time.

columns.name: tailnum
columns.comment: Aircraft tail number.

columns.name: flightnum
columns.comment: Flight number reported by the airline.

columns.name: originairportid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT airport identifier for the origin airport; foreign key to `l_airport_id.code`.

columns.name: originairportseqid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT airport sequence identifier for the origin airport at a given point in time; foreign key to `l_airport_seq_id.code`.

columns.name: origincitymarketid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT city market identifier for the origin airport; used to consolidate airports serving the same city market; foreign key to `l_city_market_id.code`.

columns.name: origin
columns.comment: Origin airport code; foreign key to `l_airport.code`.

columns.name: origincityname
columns.comment: Origin airport city name.

columns.name: originstate
columns.comment: Origin airport state or province abbreviation; foreign key to `l_state_abr_aviation.code`.

columns.name: originstatefips
columns.comment: Origin airport state FIPS code; foreign key to `l_state_fips.code`.

columns.name: originstatename
columns.comment: Origin airport state name.

columns.name: originwac
columns.comment: Origin airport World Area Code; foreign key to `l_world_area_codes.code`.

columns.name: destairportid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT airport identifier for the destination airport; foreign key to `l_airport_id.code`.

columns.name: destairportseqid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT airport sequence identifier for the destination airport at a given point in time; foreign key to `l_airport_seq_id.code`.

columns.name: destcitymarketid
columns.comment: U.S. DOT city market identifier for the destination airport; used to consolidate airports serving the same city market; foreign key to `l_city_market_id.code`.

columns.name: dest
columns.comment: Destination airport code; foreign key to `l_airport.code`.

columns.name: destcityname
columns.comment: Destination airport city name.

columns.name: deststate
columns.comment: Destination airport state or province abbreviation; foreign key to `l_state_abr_aviation.code`.

columns.name: deststatefips
columns.comment: Destination airport state FIPS code; foreign key to `l_state_fips.code`.

columns.name: deststatename
columns.comment: Destination airport state name.

columns.name: destwac
columns.comment: Destination airport World Area Code; foreign key to `l_world_area_codes.code`.

columns.name: crsdeptime
columns.comment: Scheduled departure time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: deptime
columns.comment: Actual departure time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: depdelay
columns.comment: Difference in minutes between scheduled and actual departure time; early departures are negative.

columns.name: depdelayminutes
columns.comment: Difference in minutes between scheduled and actual departure time, with early departures set to 0.

columns.name: depdel15
columns.comment: Departure delay indicator, 15 minutes or more (1 = Yes); foreign key to `l_yesno_resp.code`.

columns.name: departuredelaygroups
columns.comment: Departure delay interval group in 15-minute bins; foreign key to `l_ontime_delay_groups.code`.

columns.name: deptimeblk
columns.comment: Scheduled departure time block, hourly intervals; foreign key to `l_deparrblk.code`.

columns.name: taxiout
columns.comment: Taxi-out time, in minutes.

columns.name: wheelsoff
columns.comment: Wheels-off time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: wheelson
columns.comment: Wheels-on time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: taxiin
columns.comment: Taxi-in time, in minutes.

columns.name: crsarrtime
columns.comment: Scheduled arrival time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: arrtime
columns.comment: Actual arrival time in local hhmm format.

columns.name: arrdelay
columns.comment: Difference in minutes between scheduled and actual arrival time; early arrivals are negative.

columns.name: arrdelayminutes
columns.comment: Difference in minutes between scheduled and actual arrival time, with early arrivals set to 0.

columns.name: arrdel15
columns.comment: Arrival delay indicator, 15 minutes or more (1 = Yes); target field; foreign key to `l_yesno_resp.code`.

columns.name: arrivaldelaygroups
columns.comment: Arrival delay interval group in 15-minute bins; foreign key to `l_ontime_delay_groups.code`.

columns.name: arrtimeblk
columns.comment: Scheduled arrival time block, hourly intervals.

columns.name: cancelled
columns.comment: Cancelled flight indicator (1 = Yes); foreign key to `l_yesno_resp.code`.

columns.name: cancellationcode
columns.comment: Reason for cancellation; foreign key to `l_cancellation.code`.

columns.name: diverted
columns.comment: Diverted flight indicator (1 = Yes); foreign key to `l_yesno_resp.code`.

columns.name: crselapsedtime
columns.comment: CRS elapsed time of flight, in minutes.

columns.name: actualelapsedtime
columns.comment: Elapsed time of flight, in minutes.

columns.name: airtime
columns.comment: Flight time, in minutes.

columns.name: flights
columns.comment: Number of flights represented by the record.

columns.name: distance
columns.comment: Distance between airports, in miles.

columns.name: distancegroup
columns.comment: Distance interval group in 250-mile bins for the flight segment; foreign key to `l_distance_group_250.code`.

columns.name: carrierdelay
columns.comment: Carrier delay, in minutes.

columns.name: weatherdelay
columns.comment: Weather delay, in minutes.

columns.name: nasdelay
columns.comment: National Air System delay, in minutes.

columns.name: securitydelay
columns.comment: Security delay, in minutes.

columns.name: lateaircraftdelay
columns.comment: Late aircraft delay, in minutes.

columns.name: firstdeptime
columns.comment: First gate departure time at the origin airport.

columns.name: totaladdgtime
columns.comment: Total ground time away from gate for a gate return or cancelled flight.

columns.name: longestaddgtime
columns.comment: Longest time away from gate for a gate return or cancelled flight.

columns.name: divairportlandings
columns.comment: Number of diverted airport landings; foreign key to `l_diversions.code`.

columns.name: divreacheddest
columns.comment: Diverted flight reaching scheduled destination indicator (1 = Yes).

columns.name: divactualelapsedtime
columns.comment: Elapsed time in minutes of a diverted flight reaching scheduled destination.

columns.name: divarrdelay
columns.comment: Difference in minutes between scheduled and actual arrival time for a diverted flight reaching scheduled destination.

columns.name: divdistance
columns.comment: Distance between scheduled destination and final diverted airport, in miles.

columns.name: div1airport
columns.comment: Code of diverted airport 1.

columns.name: div1airportid
columns.comment: Airport ID of diverted airport 1; foreign key to `l_airport_id.code`.

columns.name: div1airportseqid
columns.comment: Airport sequence ID of diverted airport 1; foreign key to `l_airport_seq_id.code`.

columns.name: div1wheelson
columns.comment: Wheels-on time at diverted airport 1 in local hhmm format.

columns.name: div1totalgtime
columns.comment: Total ground time at diverted airport 1.

columns.name: div1longestgtime
columns.comment: Longest ground time at diverted airport 1.

columns.name: div1wheelsoff
columns.comment: Wheels-off time from diverted airport 1 in local hhmm format.

columns.name: div1tailnum
columns.comment: Aircraft tail number for diverted airport 1 segment.

columns.name: div2airport
columns.comment: Code of diverted airport 2.

columns.name: div2airportid
columns.comment: Airport ID of diverted airport 2; foreign key to `l_airport_id.code`.

columns.name: div2airportseqid
columns.comment: Airport sequence ID of diverted airport 2; foreign key to `l_airport_seq_id.code`.

columns.name: div2wheelson
columns.comment: Wheels-on time at diverted airport 2 in local hhmm format.

columns.name: div2totalgtime
columns.comment: Total ground time at diverted airport 2.

columns.name: div2longestgtime
columns.comment: Longest ground time at diverted airport 2.
